briefing.opening: Good day, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 8, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Accelerating Climate Crisis, Deepening Severity of Compound Disasters
sections.content: According to recent domestic and international reports, the pace of climate change has nearly doubled since 2015 [1], and compound disasters, such as droughts following heatwaves, have surged by a staggering eightfold since 2000, raising serious concerns [5]. This data clearly demonstrates that the climate crisis is no longer a distant future problem but a current threat directly impacting our daily lives. In particular, international criticism highlighting that climate change disproportionately affects women while funding for related initiatives remains insufficient underscores the unequal impact of climate change on vulnerable populations and urges the urgent development of solutions [63].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Spreading Efforts for Carbon Neutrality Implementation by Domestic Local Governments and Civil Society
sections.content: Amidst this crisis, various entities domestically continue their efforts towards carbon neutrality. Jeju Province has increased 'fishermen's allowances' to support fishing households struggling due to climate change [2, 3], and Gyeonggi Province is encouraging citizen participation by providing 'opportunity income' for carbon neutrality practices through 'tidal flat playground' experiences [6]. Yangju City's successful carbon neutrality campaign [7, 18], Yuseong-gu's practice of low-carbon eco-friendly living [8], Jeonbuk Self-Governing Province's recruitment for the 'Vehicle Carbon Neutrality Point System' [9, 16], and Chungbuk Provincial Office of Education's plan to operate carbon-neutral schools in all schools [10] demonstrate widespread changes across local communities. In particular, Incheon Environmental Corporation is taking its first steps towards carbon neutrality with citizens [11] and recruiting supporters for AI-powered promotional content creation [13, 14], thereby encouraging active participation. Such initiatives as Samcheok City's hydrogen electric vehicle dissemination [15] and Jeju Province's recruitment of ESG investment coastal ecosystem restoration companies [19] suggest diverse approaches to carbon neutrality.

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Renewable Energy Investment and Climate Tech Innovation Trends
sections.content: A 'Climate Tech' innovation alliance for growth-oriented carbon neutrality has been launched [4, 17], raising expectations for overcoming the climate crisis through technology. In particular, Hana Bank and Korea Southern Power are accelerating the expansion of carbon-neutral infrastructure through full-cycle cooperation in renewable energy, including the joint development of offshore wind power projects [12, 20, 23-25, 27-32, 36-41, 43-57]. This emphasizes the importance of productive finance for a major transition to renewable energy and illustrates one aspect of the banking sector's expanding social role [20, 33]. Furthermore, large-scale investments are continuing, such as Taean County signing a 1 trillion won-scale offshore wind renewable energy investment agreement with Vina Group [34, 35], and benefits for the renewable energy sector are anticipated due to increasing RE100 demand [26]. Namwon City's expansion of its new and renewable energy convergence support project [21] and Boryeong citizens' search for energy transition solutions [22] also demonstrate active efforts at the local level to expand renewable energy.

sections.title: ⚠️ Reversal in International Climate Policy and New Challenges
sections.content: Internationally, conflicting movements regarding climate action are observed. The U.S. federal government is turning its back on climate change issues, with some regions like Colorado emphasizing their independent efforts [61]. Meanwhile, the slowdown in global EV market sales is leading to layoffs of factory workers at SK On [58], illustrating the challenges of the EV transition, and concerns are also being raised that climate change could negatively impact EV batteries [62]. Furthermore, an analysis of 3,000 newspaper editorials reveals a trend in climate reporting where national newspapers primarily cover nuclear power, while regional ones focus on renewable energy, suggesting a bias in climate discourse [42]. Concurrently, with Professor Alex Wang discussing China and climate action at a UCLA Law event [59], continuous attention to the climate policy directions of major countries is required.

briefing.closing: This concludes today's climate briefing for March 8, 2026. We hope that active responses and innovative efforts, both domestically and internationally, will continue amidst the accelerating climate crisis, and we will bring you more in-depth climate-related news in our next briefing.

summary.phenomenon: SK On's plant in Georgia, USA, laid off over one-third of its employees on the 6th (local time) due to the reduction in electric vehicle (EV) business by its client, Ford. This occurred amidst a continued slowdown in global EV market sales.
summary.cause: The causes are the reduction in electric vehicle (EV) business by its client, Ford, and the ongoing slowdown in global EV market sales.
